classData.comment:
/**
 * Top-level Map substrate files (turn-loaded; AGENTS.md is mechanically capped at
 * 24,576 bytes by `check-substrate-size.mjs:PER_FILE_LIMIT_BYTES` with truncation).
 * Anchor-tag scaffolding here directly compresses future instruction budget.
 */
classData.description: Top-level Map substrate files (turn-loaded; AGENTS.md is mechanically capped at 24,576 bytes by check-substrate-size.mjs:PER_FILE_LIMIT_BYTES with truncation). Anchor-tag scaffolding here directly compresses future instruction budget.
classData.name: TOP_LEVEL_MAP_FILES

classData.comment:
/**
 * Skill substrate (skill-loaded; loaded at skill invocation, payloads can grow but
 * carry per-invocation cost). Subject to the operator forward-rule.
 */
classData.description: Skill substrate (skill-loaded; loaded at skill invocation, payloads can grow but carry per-invocation cost). Subject to the operator forward-rule.
classData.name: SKILL_PREFIX

classData.comment:
/**
 * Agent OS substrate (mixed turn-load + skill-load + reference-load). Includes
 * `AGENTS_ATLAS.md` (World Atlas Map) and the ADR set under `decisions/`. Subject to
 * the text-only substrate numbering convention.
 */
classData.description: Agent OS substrate (mixed turn-load + skill-load + reference-load). Includes AGENTS_ATLAS.md (World Atlas Map) and the ADR set under decisions/. Subject to the text-only substrate numbering convention.
classData.name: AGENTOS_PREFIX

classData.comment:
/**
 * Only markdown files participate; manifests, JSON, and other non-prose substrate
 * cannot meaningfully carry anchor-tag damage.
 */
classData.description: Only markdown files participate; manifests, JSON, and other non-prose substrate cannot meaningfully carry anchor-tag damage.
classData.name: SCOPE_SUFFIX

classData.comment:
/**
 * Regex for `<a id="...">` / `<a name="...">` HTML anchor-tag insertions. Tolerates
 * intervening attributes (e.g. `<a class="x" id="foo">`) and whitespace. Case-insensitive.
 * Anchored on `<a\b` word-boundary to avoid false matches against `<abbr>`, `<area>`, etc.
 * The `\s(?:id|name)\s*=` clause requires the attribute keyword to be a discrete token,
 * so `<a href="...">` regular hyperlinks (no id/name attribute) are NOT flagged.
 */
classData.description: Regex for <a id="..."> / <a name="..."> HTML anchor-tag insertions. Tolerates intervening attributes (e.g. <a class="x" id="foo">) and whitespace. Case-insensitive. Anchored on <a\b word-boundary to avoid false matches against <abbr>, <area>, etc. The \s(?:id|name)\s*= clause requires the attribute keyword to be a discrete token, so <a href="..."> regular hyperlinks (no id/name attribute) are NOT flagged.
classData.name: ANCHOR_TAG_PATTERN

classData.comment:
/**
 * Same-line classification cues that explicitly exempt a line per the anchor-tag exception
 * contract. `historical` / `archaeology` / `errata` preserve substrate-archaeology
 * references; `rendered-consumer` covers the narrow operator-approved exception for
 * specific rendered-consumer needs. Case-insensitive substring match.
 */
classData.description: Same-line classification cues that explicitly exempt a line per the anchor-tag exception contract. historical / archaeology / errata preserve substrate-archaeology references; rendered-consumer covers the narrow operator-approved exception for specific rendered-consumer needs. Case-insensitive substring match.
classData.name: EXEMPTION_MARKERS

classData.comment:
/**
 * Parses simple `--base <branch>` / `--base=<branch>` CLI args. Default base is
 * `origin/dev` to match `lint-skill-manifest.mjs` and CI workflow conventions.
 * @param {string[]} argv
 * @returns {{base: string, help: boolean}} `help` only set when requested.
 */
classData.description: Parses simple --base <branch> / --base=<branch> CLI args. Default base is origin/dev to match lint-skill-manifest.mjs and CI workflow conventions.
classData.name: parseArgs

classData.comment:
/**
 * Runs `git diff --unified=0 <base>...HEAD` and returns the raw diff text.
 * Pathspecs cover the union of all in-scope substrate roots so the lint catches
 * insertions across Map + skill + Agent OS files in a single diff pass.
 * Empty string when no diff or when base ref doesn't exist locally.
 * @param {string} base Git ref to compare HEAD against.
 * @returns {string}
 */
classData.description: Runs git diff --unified=0 <base>...HEAD and returns the raw diff text. Pathspecs cover the union of all in-scope substrate roots so the lint catches insertions across Map + skill + Agent OS files in a single diff pass. Empty string when no diff or when base ref doesn't exist locally.
classData.name: gitDiff

classData.comment:
/**
 * Parses a unified diff into a flat list of added-line records. Each record carries
 * the file path (relative to repo root), the new-file line number, and the line text
 * without the leading `+`.
 *
 * Diff header parsing is intentionally minimal — we only need the new-file path
 * (`+++ b/...`) and the `@@ -... +newStart[,newCount] @@` hunk locator to count
 * lines forward inside each hunk.
 *
 * @param {string} diffText
 * @returns {Array<{file: string, line: number, text: string}>}
 */
classData.description:
Parses a unified diff into a flat list of added-line records. Each record carries the file path (relative to repo root), the new-file line number, and the line text without the leading +.

Diff header parsing is intentionally minimal — we only need the new-file path (+++ b/...) and the @@ -... +newStart[,newCount] @@ hunk locator to count lines forward inside each hunk.

names: Array.<{file: string, line: number, text: string}>

classData.name: parseAddedLines

classData.comment:
/**
 * Returns true when the line text contains a same-line exemption marker per
 * the anchor-tag exception contract. Match is case-insensitive substring; intentionally
 * permissive so authors can mark lines without learning a strict syntax.
 * @param {string} text
 * @returns {boolean}
 */
classData.description: Returns true when the line text contains a same-line exemption marker per the anchor-tag exception contract. Match is case-insensitive substring; intentionally permissive so authors can mark lines without learning a strict syntax.
classData.name: isExempt

classData.comment:
/**
 * Returns true when the file path falls within the lint scope. Covers turn-loaded Map
 * substrate (AGENTS.md / AGENTS_STARTUP.md / ANTIGRAVITY_RULES.md), skill substrate
 * (.agents/skills/**\/*.md), and Agent OS substrate (learn/agentos/**\/*.md).
 * Manifests, JSON, and other non-markdown substrate are out of scope.
 * @param {string} filePath
 * @returns {boolean}
 */
classData.description: Returns true when the file path falls within the lint scope. Covers turn-loaded Map substrate (AGENTS.md / AGENTS_STARTUP.md / ANTIGRAVITY_RULES.md), skill substrate (.agents/skills//*.md), and Agent OS substrate (learn/agentos//*.md). Manifests, JSON, and other non-markdown substrate are out of scope.
classData.name: isInScope

classData.comment:
/**
 * Inspects a single added line for `<a id>` / `<a name>` HTML anchor-tag insertions.
 * Returns an array of violation records (empty when clean or when the line carries an
 * exemption marker per `EXEMPTION_MARKERS`).
 * @param {{file: string, line: number, text: string}} record
 * @returns {Array<{file: string, line: number, match: string, text: string}>}
 */
classData.description: Inspects a single added line for <a id> / <a name> HTML anchor-tag insertions. Returns an array of violation records (empty when clean or when the line carries an exemption marker per EXEMPTION_MARKERS).

names: Array.<{file: string, line: number, match: string, text: string}>

classData.name: findViolationsInLine

classData.description: Pure-function lint entry: feed it diff text, get back violations. Exported for unit testing without invoking git or the filesystem.

names: Array.<{file: string, line: number, match: string, text: string}>

classData.name: lintDiff

classData.description: CLI entry. Returns numeric exit code so unit tests can drive it without triggering process.exit.
classData.name: runLint
